author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
title: Epilogue.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: As for what they say, that some men, by the use of their reason, have lived, and do live, in this world without sin, we should wish that it were true, we should strive to make it true, we should pray that it be true; but, at the same time, we should confess that it is not yet true.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: For to those who wish and strive and worthily pray for this result, whatever sins remain in them are daily remitted because we sincerely pray, “Forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: Whosoever shall deny that this prayer is in this life necessary for every righteous man who knows and does the will of God, except the one Saint of saints, greatly errs, and is utterly incapable of pleasing Him whom he praises.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: Moreover, if he supposes himself to be such a character, “he deceives himself, and the truth is not in him,”—for no other reason than that he thinks what is false.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: That Physician, then, who is not needed by the sound, but by the sick, knows how to heal us, and by healing to perfect us unto eternal life; and He does not in this world take away death, although inflicted because of sin, from those whose sins He remits, in order that they may enter on their conflict, and overcome the fear of death with full sincerity of faith.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: In some cases, too, He declines to help even His righteous servants, so long as they are capable of still higher elevation, to the attainment of a perfect righteousness, in order that (while in His sight no man living is justified) we may always feel it to be our duty to give Him thanks for mercifully bearing with us, and so, by holy humility, be healed of that first cause of all our failings, even the swellings of pride.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: This letter, as my intention first sketched it, was to have been a short one; it has grown into a lengthy book.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Merits and Forgiveness of Sins, and on the Baptism of Infants.
verses.text: Would that it were as perfect as it has at last become complete!
